qa_pairs.question: How can one display a single progress bar when using `rsync` to transfer a large number of small files?
qa_pairs.answer: When transferring a large number of smaller files with `rsync`, you can display a single progress bar representing the transfer progress of all files by using the `--info=progress2` option.

qa_pairs.question: How do you transfer files from a remote system to a local project directory using `rsync`?
qa_pairs.answer: To transfer files from a remote system into a local project directory using `rsync`, you can use a command similar to: `rsync -avzh --no-g --no-p someuser@nibi.alliancecan.ca:REMOTENAME ~/projects/def-professor/someuser/somedir/`. In this command, `REMOTENAME` is the path to the directory or file on the remote system, and `~/projects/def-professor/someuser/somedir/` is the destination path on the local system.
